place: Erma CDP, New Jersey, New Jersey

facts.text: Erma sits within Lower Township, a community incorporated in 1798 that also includes historic towns like Cold Spring, Fishing Creek, and Villas.
Historic Erma hosts landmarks such as the Fishing Creek School, Naval Air Station Wildwood, Cold Spring Village, and the Cape May Lighthouse.
At Sunset Beach, the Atlantus—a concrete ship from World War I—stands as a striking maritime landmark.
Cold Spring Presbyterian Church preserves more than three centuries of Cape May County history within its historic walls.
Lower Township contains two historic African American cemeteries, including Mount Zion Cemetery, linked to the Underground Railroad and Harriet Tubman’s era.
The Lower Township Police Department station, though bearing an Erma address, actually sits outside the CDP boundaries.
